title: Aureline connected-account registry: human account, installation / app / bot, delegated credential, project-scoped grant, policy-injected service identity, acting-identity badge, and invalidation-event vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the connected-account registry frozen in docs/providers/connected_account_registry_contract.md. The registry is separate from the local user profile, the secret broker, and the settings registry; it names the identity a provider-linked action will execute as. The connected_provider_record family is exported by schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json (ADR-0010); this schema exports the constituent link / grant / credential / identity / badge / invalidation records the provider record points at. Seven record kinds are exported: human_account_link_record, installation_or_app_grant_record, delegated_credential_binding_record, project_scoped_grant_record, policy_injected_service_identity_record, acting_identity_badge_record, and account_invalidation_event_record. Raw tokens, raw install secrets, raw delegated-token bodies, raw policy-injector material, raw URLs, and raw provider payloads never cross this boundary; records carry opaque refs, fingerprints, and structured fields only. The eventual connected-provider crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new actor class, badge surface, badge-label class, invalidation cause class, downgrade-action class, or revocation-trigger class is additive-minor and requires a connected_account_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

connected_account_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this connected-account vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / queue-review rollups / admin-reconciliation consoles. Raw URLs, raw tokens, raw callback bodies, raw delegated-token bodies, and raw policy-injector material never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show on desktop, CLI, notifications, provider-linked headers, queue-review surfaces, and support exports.

provider_actor_class.description: Frozen provider-actor class vocabulary reused from schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. A generic 'Connected' state is forbidden on every product, CLI, notification, provider-linked header, support-export, queue-review, and audit surface; 'unknown_actor_class' is repair-only and MUST NOT be collapsed with any other class.

trust_posture.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state the link, grant, credential, identity, badge, or invalidation event was captured under.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class reused from ADR-0007 and ADR-0010 redaction posture. Raw tokens, raw install secrets, raw delegated-token bodies, raw policy-injector material, raw URLs, and raw provider payloads never cross this boundary regardless of class.

health_state.description: Provider-side health state the link, grant, credential, or identity is currently observed under. Reused from schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json.

verification_state_class.description: Verification posture for the underlying provider-side principal. A link whose verification has expired or failed MUST NOT silently keep claiming a fresh verified state.

review_status_class.description: Admin / org review status for an installation / app / bot grant. A grant MUST NOT silently flip from pending to approved without naming a review record.

suspension_or_revocation_state_class.description: Suspension / revocation state for an installation grant, project-scoped grant, delegated credential, or policy-injected service identity. Names exactly one cause; silent retry under a different cause is forbidden.

token_type_class.description: Token-shape class for a delegated credential binding. Raw token bodies live behind the ADR-0007 broker; this field carries shape only, never raw material.

projection_mode_class.description: ADR-0007 secret-broker projection mode declared on the credential binding. external_projection_forbidden MUST be set on signing-class credentials by default.

storage_class.description: ADR-0007 storage class for a delegated credential. Raw bytes live behind the broker; this field names where the broker is authoritative for the binding.

materialisation_scope_class.description: Lifetime scope for a policy-injected service identity. Per-call is the safest default; broader scopes MUST be admin-approved and re-validated on policy-epoch roll.

linked_account_kind_class.description: Sub-classification of a human account link. A surface that surfaces multiple links MUST distinguish primary from secondary and MUST NOT silently publish under a deactivated link.

install_kind_class.description: Install / app / bot kind class. user_install is bound to a single principal; org_install and tenant_install survive human session boundaries and require separate review.

label_surface_class.description: Surface family the acting-identity badge / label is rendered into. Every protected surface that surfaces an actor class MUST cite at least one label_surface_class so 'you', 'install', 'bot', 'delegated', and 'policy-injected service' never collapse into one generic state.

label_class.description: Frozen acting-identity label vocabulary. Every protected surface renders exactly one label class; widening, redefining, or aliasing a label class is forbidden.

tone_class.description: Tone the badge / label is rendered with. unknown_actor_repair_label MUST be paired with warning_repair_required; widening tone is forbidden.

invalidation_cause_class.description: Frozen cause class an invalidation event names. Every account_invalidation_event_record names exactly one cause; silent retry under a different cause is forbidden.

downgrade_action_class.description: Frozen downgrade action invalidation triggers. Surfaces MUST render the named downgrade and MUST NOT invent surface-local downgrades outside this set.

policy_owner_class.description: Who owns the policy bundle that governs this connected provider link / grant / credential / identity at observation time.

object_identity.description: Provider-side identity an invalidation event or badge record references. Extends the ADR-0010 set with principal_subject, install_target, and tenant_or_org so account-side events render without inventing a parallel vocabulary.

display_label.description: Human-readable display label used on every desktop / CLI / notification / provider-linked header / support-export surface. Never a raw token, raw email body, or raw provider payload; the label is the user-visible projection only.

provider_side_principal_ref.description: Opaque ref to the provider-side principal subject. Empty string when the provider has not yet returned a stable subject and the link is still in pending_verification.

granted_scope_refs.description: Opaque refs to the provider-declared scope rows the link grants. Raw provider scope tokens never appear here.

allOf.description: verified MUST carry a non-empty provider_side_principal_ref.
verification_expired and verification_failed MUST NOT be rendered as authoritative; the link MUST also carry a non-healthy health_state on every rendered surface.

human_account_link_record.description: One human account link on a connected provider. Names the human principal Aureline acts as for human_account-class actions. Prevents 'signed in' from implying unbounded authorization; verification_state_class, granted_scope_refs, and session_expires_at all degrade visibly.

granted_operations.description: Opaque refs to the provider-declared operation rows this grant admits (for example, 'pull_request:write', 'check_run:write'). Raw provider scope strings live behind the broker; this field carries opaque ids the registry resolves at the narrowest possible projection boundary.

org_restriction_refs.description: Opaque refs to org / tenant restrictions the install is narrowed by.

review_decision_ref.description: Opaque ref to the admin / org review decision row that admitted (or rejected) the install.

allOf.description: rejected, expired, and revoked installs MUST carry a non-healthy health_state.
approved review_status MUST cite a review_decision_ref unless the install kind is review_not_required at the provider.

installation_or_app_grant_record.description: One installation / app / bot grant on a connected provider. Install grants outlive human sessions; the record keeps install_kind_class, scope_refs, granted_operations, review_status_class, and suspension_or_revocation_state_class visible so the install never silently aliases a human account.

issuer_ref.description: Opaque ref to the upstream identity issuer (workspace identity provider, federation root, policy injector). Raw issuer URLs and raw discovery documents never appear here.

delegated_principal_ref.description: Opaque ref to the principal subject the delegated credential is forwarded for. Empty string only when token_type_class is delegated_workload_assertion and no human principal applies.

credential_alias_ref.description: ADR-0007 broker handle. The handle resolves to projected material at the narrowest possible projection boundary; raw token bytes never appear in the registry.

rotation_path_ref.description: Opaque ref to the rotation path the broker uses to refresh this binding. Empty string only for short-lived per-call bindings with no rotation.

allOf.description: Expired, revoked, and suspended bindings MUST carry a non-healthy health_state on every surface.
external_projection_forbidden MUST be set when token_type_class is delegated_signed_jwt or delegated_workload_assertion of signing-class material; surfaces MAY narrow further but MUST NOT widen.

delegated_credential_binding_record.description: One delegated credential binding on a connected provider. The binding names the broker handle (credential_alias_ref) the action will project through; raw delegated-token bodies live behind ADR-0007 and never enter this record.

scope_ref.description: Opaque ref to the project / repo / tenant scope row the grant narrows to.

scope_label.description: Reviewable label for the scope (project, repo, tenant). Surfaces MUST render this; collapsing the label into the parent actor class is forbidden.

parent_actor_class.description: Actor class the project-scoped grant is narrowed from (typically human_account or installation_or_app_grant). project_scoped_grant and policy_injected_service_identity MUST NOT be self-parents.

parent_subject_ref.description: Opaque ref to the parent subject the grant narrows from.

allOf.description: parent_actor_class MUST NOT be project_scoped_grant or policy_injected_service_identity (no self-parenting).

project_scoped_grant_record.description: One project / repo / tenant narrowing on a connected provider. The grant record keeps the narrowing visible rather than letting it hide under the parent actor class.

issued_by_policy_ref.description: Opaque ref to the policy bundle row that materialises this identity.

policy_epoch.description: Policy epoch at materialisation. A policy-epoch roll between materialisation and use re-evaluates the identity; silent continued-use is forbidden.

policy_injected_service_identity_record.description: One policy-injected service identity on a connected provider. Carries an explicit policy_epoch so policy-epoch rolls invalidate cached effective scopes deterministically; silent continued-use after a roll is forbidden.

display_label.description: User-visible label string projected onto the named surfaces (for example, 'You — ahmedyounis@github.com', 'Install — aureline-ci-bot', 'Delegated for ahmedyounis@github.com', 'Policy-injected service — release-publisher@policy-epoch:42'). Raw tokens, raw URLs, and raw provider payloads never appear.

secondary_label.description: Optional secondary-line label (host, scope, expiry hint). Empty string when the surface only renders the primary label.

scope_summary.description: Optional short scope summary (project, repo, tenant). Empty string when no narrowing applies.

linked_record_ref.description: Opaque ref to the human_account_link / installation_or_app_grant / delegated_credential_binding / project_scoped_grant / policy_injected_service_identity record the badge is rendering. Empty string only for unknown_actor_repair_label badges that exist solely to route to repair.

allOf.description: actor_class human_account MUST render label_class you_label.
actor_class installation_or_app_grant MUST render label_class install_label or bot_label.
actor_class delegated_user_token MUST render label_class delegated_label.
actor_class project_scoped_grant MUST render label_class project_scoped_grant_label.
actor_class policy_injected_service_identity MUST render label_class policy_injected_service_label.
actor_class unknown_actor_class MUST render label_class unknown_actor_repair_label and tone warning_repair_required.
unknown_actor_repair_label MUST be paired with tone warning_repair_required.

acting_identity_badge_record.description: One acting-identity badge / label rendered on a desktop, CLI, notification, provider-linked header, or support-export surface. Freezes the label vocabulary so 'you', 'install', 'bot', 'delegated', and 'policy-injected service' never collapse into one generic state.

subject_record_ref.description: Opaque ref to the human_account_link / installation_or_app_grant / delegated_credential_binding / project_scoped_grant / policy_injected_service_identity record the invalidation applies to.

invalidated_dependent_refs.description: Opaque refs to dependent approval-ticket, browser-handoff-packet, publish-later queue-item, and provider-scope-resolution-result records that are invalidated by this event.

repair_hook_ref.description: Opaque ref to the repair hook the surface routes to (account reselection, browser recovery, step-up authenticator, admin reconciliation). Empty string only when downgrade_action_class is no_downgrade_required.

allOf.description: Every non-no-downgrade event MUST cite a repair_hook_ref so the surface has a concrete reopen path.
host_mismatch, tenant_switch, and org_membership_loss MUST NOT downgrade to no_downgrade_required; the action MUST visibly degrade.
credential_expiry MUST apply only to delegated_credential_binding subjects.

account_invalidation_event_record.description: An observable invalidation / downgrade event on a connected-account record. Names the cause (revocation, suspension, host mismatch, tenant switch, org-membership loss, expired delegated credential, policy-epoch roll, trust-state downgrade) and the typed downgrade action surfaces MUST render. Silent continued-use is forbidden; every non-no-downgrade event names a repair hook.
